title: Did they erase Shinjis memory
wikitext:
[]: Did they erase Shinji´s memory?             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon Jun 24, 2013 7:29 pm Sorry if this is related to the fact that I´m not a native english speaker. When Fuyutsuki, while playing shogi, tells Shinji that his memory was erased, does he just mean that he forgot, does he mean that his memorys were actually erased with some procedure, or is the sentence vague?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Jun 24, 2013 8:31 pm ...this is kinda open/vague, but... Personally, I think Fuyutsuki was speaking figuratively; No reason to change something relatable like repression into random MIB procedures, esp since there's no reason to a) bring a kid who's not supposed to see anyway, esp if Yui still anticipated the result. b) bothering with doing a memorywipe on a three year old. Who'd believe him? especially since the script apparently labels the closeup shot of Yui during the ExperimentI as a "recollection/memory" - most likely of Shinji's. His expressions/actions do match the idea of it all surging from whatever dark corner it was confined in - He notably includes Yui in the following list of people he can't trust anymore, ("Father... Mother... Misato-san....") so he may even have put two and two together and realized that Yui deliberately left him, but that's admittedly just speculation related to a similar occurence in EoTV ("Why do I always have to keep fighting? Tell me! mother! - adressed at eva 01. Ep 25!Shinji, at least, knew who put him in this clusterfuck... Not sure his EoE counterpart ever grasped that. Shinji in general had this somewhat skewered view of her as the victim and Gendo as the bad guy who killed her, but I guess it was easy to imagine her as a saint with her not present. You don't speak ill of the dead, and since Gendo left him, and in his mind, put him in a situation where ppl would call him the son of a murderer, constructing ast least Yui into a good person may be a simple survival mechanism.), it could be as simple as that he learnt she was in the EVA and thus associated all the horrible EVA-related things he experienced with her.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Jun 24, 2013 10:03 pm I think it's just a natural translation mistake. A word or phrasing in Japanese that when taken over to English means just that "Your memory was erased" but more likely means "You've blocked it from your memory". But I'm a gaijin who doesn't speak lick of Japanese except for the lines I remember phonetically from Eva so I'm most likely wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jun 25, 2013 4:00 am There was a debate about that line, and IIRC Reichu said that the Japanese wording left no doubt that Shinji's memories were deliberately erased by a third party. The fact that the shot of Yui during the contact experiment is labelled as a "memory" in the script can mean that by talking about it, Fuyutsuki "unlocked" part of these memories and Shinji had a flash of the scene. And remember that it's not the first time we see Gendo and Fuyutsuki talking about brain manipulation : in 1.0 when they wondered if Shinji will accept to participate in Operation Yashima, they were contemplating using brainwashing on Shinji if he refused to forces him, and using Rei for the task was proposed for if that plan failed ! So yeah, I really think that Gendo don't have any qualms to use brainwashing on his son, and already did it before! Also I'm sure that this plot point will have it's importance later in FINAL, Shinji must have witnessed something during the contact experiment that put his role in SEELE and/or Gendo's plans in jeopardy.

[]:             Quote Post by Rahxephon » Tue Jun 25, 2013 11:52 am I was under the impression that Shinji was traumatized because he loved his mom so much and as we saw in the series she really was a good mom while she was alive and spent much time with him, perhaps it was so traumatizing for a 3 year old to see his memories were erased. Besides who here honestly remembers ANYTHING from when they are 3. I know I don't. I have very few memories of my ages 6 - 10 besides school, playing with my friends and important life events but not much. although I certainly wouldnt put it past gendo to erase his 3 year old sons memories if he thought it would help with his plan. perhaps he thought if Shinji remembered he wouldnt be able to use him to pilot the eva later in his life? but even this is kind of shoddy because who knows if Shinji knew eva unit 1 was his mother he might have been more open to getting inside of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Jun 25, 2013 1:37 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: I think it's just a natural translation mistake. A word or phrasing in Japanese that when taken over to English means just that "Your memory was erased" but more likely means "You've blocked it from your memory". But I'm a gaijin who doesn't speak lick of Japanese except for the lines I remember phonetically from Eva so I'm most likely wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Jun 25, 2013 7:05 pm I'm in favour of more conventional brainwashing/hypnosis. The very fact that Shinji eventually remembers after Fuyutsuki reveals the information suggests that it has only been buried. The idea of a brainwashed Shinji, particularly in light of Gendo's intentions for his relationship with Rei, is an intriguing notion. Is Shinji being unconsciously manipulated? Was his awakening of Eva-01 a preprogrammed notion? His take over of Eva-13's controls? Is Shinji Gendo's own personal Manchurian Candidate?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jun 26, 2013 3:48 am It's possible that Gendo though that Shinji remembering what happened to Yui would threaten his willingness to pilot EVA-01, after all it would be hard to convince him to pilot a giant robot when last time he saw it he "devoured" his mother! And even if he was only three years old, seeing his mother being absorbed by some sort of giant bio-mechanical monstrosity cut open in the torso area is not something you forget easily! Besides, Kozo wouldn't have used a term as specific as " sakujo " - eliminate, erase etc... if what he meant is that Shinji simply suppressed his memories of the event, he would have said something like "Your memories were repressed". EDIT : I splitted the big speculation part of my post in the "Gendo's Master Plan" thread, I think it had better its place there.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jun 26, 2013 4:10 am ElMariachi wrote: EDIT : I splitted the big speculation part of my post in the "Gendo's Master Plan" thread, I think he had better its place there.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Wed Jun 26, 2013 8:30 pm Thanks for the answers! So it seems we can't be entirely sure? I don't think this will be adressed in 4.0... :/

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Thu Jun 27, 2013 6:32 am Shinji has this habit of trying to recognize the ceiling he sees when he wakes up. That's not really something normal people do after a night's sleep. I suspect he's woken up with no recollection of when he fell unconscious/asleep many times - far more than shown in Rebuild - and that's where the habit stems from. "Oh, I don't remember falling asleep. Do I know this ceiling?" It's rather creepy if you read into it...

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Jun 27, 2013 6:45 am He already had this habit in NGE, at the beginning it was to showcase that Shinji began a new page of his life by moving in an unknown place for him(Misato's apartment), and latter to show how often he ended in NERV's hospital, since past some time he began to instantly recognize the hospital ceiling. But I admit that your idea of using this habit from NGE and expand it to a sign of regular brainwashing. But even then I don't think that Shinji has been literally "programmed" by Gendo to act the way he did : one of Evangelion's core themes is Shinji trying to surpass himself and his inherent flaws to grow into a better people. If it's revealed that most of his character and flaws came from brainwashing that would defeat the purpose of trying to better oneself. I think that Gendo just brainwashed Shinji once, after Yui's "death", and after that molded his son by manipulation, by abandoning him and putting him in the care of someone out of the family(Shinji's teacher) to transform him into someone who crave for attention, making it more likely that he will try to bond with Rei, who will unconsciously make him remember his other.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jun 27, 2013 4:16 pm ElMariachi wrote: I think that Gendo just brainwashed Shinji once, after Yui's "death", and after that molded his son by manipulation, by abandoning him and putting him in the care of someone out of the family(Shinji's teacher) to transform him into someone who crave for attention, making it more likely that he will try to bond with Rei, who will unconsciously make him remember his other.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Thu Jun 27, 2013 4:21 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Rebuild Gendo is simultaneously more informative and more incomprehensible. I still lean towards Gendo having personal difficulties with Shinji, but it's impossible to say.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jun 27, 2013 8:33 pm Shinoyami65 wrote: It is possible that Gendo did want to connect with Shinji but felt that he could not, as we see that he was also going to attend the dinner being held by Rei, but was forced to turn around and head for Nerv HQ when Bardiel emerged.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jun 28, 2013 3:41 am In my opinion, unlike in NGE where Gendo had to reluctantly include Shinji in his plans because Rei was KO by the time Sachiel attacked, here in NME it's clear that Shinji was a part of his plans since the beginning, but it comes as a contradiction with Yui's request to take care of Shinji(strange thing by the way, did Yui told Gendo what she was about to do?) Gendo probably told himself that once Shinji's role will be over and everyone will be reunited, Shinji will understand why Gendo acted that way, and that he will have the whole eternity to make up for it. Yui's apparition probably stirred some feeling of guilt in Gendo, remembered him that even if it's ultimately to reunite the whole Ikari family, he's still an huge douche to his son and manipulating him, and probably accepted out of guilt toward Yui and to ease a little his conscience. Which make his behavior in 3.0 really tragic : whatever plan he ahd at the end of 2.0 that would "soon" come to completion as obviously gone wrong, making him become even more obsessed by reuniting with Yui with the passing years necessary for his contingency plan, to the point that he see absolutely everything and everyone only as tools for his scenario, consider the Reis as only machines to pilot Mark.09, and completely forgot his promise to Yui, seeing how he put his clueless son right in the middle of 4I and having to fight his former friends. And it's even worse if its revealed that he really was behind the event that drove Shinji insane(beginning with Toji's shirt "coincidently" arriving at Shinji's room, and culminating with Fuyutsuki's revelations)

[]:             Quote Post by Crow » Fri Jun 28, 2013 5:14 am ElMariachi wrote: Yui's request to take care of Shinji(strange thing by the way, did Yui told Gendo what she was about to do?)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jun 28, 2013 8:30 am ElMariachi wrote: Gendo probably told himself that once Shinji's role will be over and everyone will be reunited, Shinji will understand why Gendo acted that way, and that he will have the whole eternity to make up for it.

But I admit that your idea of using this habit from NGE and expand it to a sign of regular brainwashing. But even then I don't think that Shinji has been literally "programmed" by Gendo to act the way he did : one of Evangelion's core themes is Shinji trying to surpass himself and his inherent flaws to grow into a better people. If it's revealed that most of his character and flaws came from brainwashing that would defeat the purpose of trying to better oneself. I think that Gendo just brainwashed Shinji once, after Yui's "death", and after that molded his son by manipulation, by abandoning him and putting him in the care of someone out of the family(Shinji's teacher) to transform him into someone who crave for attention, making it more likely that he will try to bond with Rei, who will unconsciously make him remember his other.

Gendo probably told himself that once Shinji's role will be over and everyone will be reunited, Shinji will understand why Gendo acted that way, and that he will have the whole eternity to make up for it. Yui's apparition probably stirred some feeling of guilt in Gendo, remembered him that even if it's ultimately to reunite the whole Ikari family, he's still an huge douche to his son and manipulating him, and probably accepted out of guilt toward Yui and to ease a little his conscience.
